events.javadoc: Called when an anvil is damaged from being used
events.name: AnvilDamagedEvent

events.javadoc: Called when a lingering potion applies it's effects. Happens once every 5 ticks
events.name: AreaEffectCloudApplyEvent

events.javadoc: This event will sometimes fire synchronously, depending on how it was triggered. The constructor provides a boolean to indicate if the event was fired synchronously or asynchronously. When asynchronous, this event can be called from any thread, sans the main thread, and has limited access to the API. If a player is the direct cause of this event by an incoming packet, this event will be asynchronous. If a plugin triggers this event by compelling a player to chat, this event will be synchronous. Care should be taken to check Event.isAsynchronous() and treat the event appropriately.
events.name: AsyncPlayerChatEvent

events.javadoc: Stores details for players attempting to log in. This event is asynchronous, and not run using main thread.
events.name: AsyncPlayerPreLoginEvent

events.javadoc: Allows plugins to compute tab completion results asynchronously. If this event provides completions, then the standard synchronous process will not be fired to populate the results. However, the synchronous TabCompleteEvent will fire with the Async results. Only 1 process will be allowed to provide completions, the Async Event, or the standard process.
events.name: AsyncTabCompleteEvent

events.javadoc: Called when a bat attempts to sleep or wake up from its slumber. If a Bat Toggle Sleep event is cancelled, the Bat will not toggle its sleep state.
events.name: BatToggleSleepEvent

events.javadoc: Called when a beacon effect is being applied to a player.
events.name: BeaconEffectEvent

events.javadoc: Called when a block is broken by a player. If you wish to have the block drop experience, you must set the experience value above 0. By default, experience will be set in the event if: The player is not in creative or adventure mode The player can loot the block (ie: does not destroy it completely, by using the correct tool) The player does not have silk touch The block drops experience in vanilla Minecraft Note: Plugins wanting to simulate a traditional block drop should set the block to air and utilize their own methods for determining what the default drop for the block being broken is and what to do about it, if anything. If a Block Break event is cancelled, the block will not break and experience will not drop.
events.name: BlockBreakEvent

events.javadoc: Called when a block is destroyed as a result of being burnt by fire. If a Block Burn event is cancelled, the block will not be destroyed as a result of being burnt by fire.
events.name: BlockBurnEvent

events.javadoc: Called when we try to place a block, to see if we can build it here or not. Note: The Block returned by getBlock() is the block we are trying to place on, not the block we are trying to place. If you want to figure out what is being placed, use getMaterial() instead.
events.name: BlockCanBuildEvent

events.javadoc: Called when an ItemStack is successfully cooked in a block.
events.name: BlockCookEvent

events.javadoc: Called when a block is damaged by a player. If a Block Damage event is cancelled, the block will not be damaged.
events.name: BlockDamageEvent

events.javadoc: Fired anytime the server intends to 'destroy' a block through some triggering reason. This does not fire anytime a block is set to air, but only with more direct triggers such as physics updates, pistons, Entities changing blocks, commands set to "Destroy". This event is associated with the game playing a sound effect at the block in question, when something can be described as "intend to destroy what is there", Events such as leaves decaying, pistons retracting (where the block is moving), does NOT fire this event.
events.name: BlockDestroyEvent

events.javadoc: Called when an equippable item is dispensed from a block and equipped on a nearby entity. If a Block Dispense Armor event is cancelled, the equipment will not be equipped on the target entity.
events.name: BlockDispenseArmorEvent

events.javadoc: Called when an item is dispensed from a block. If a Block Dispense event is cancelled, the block will not dispense the item.
events.name: BlockDispenseEvent

events.javadoc: Called if a block broken by a player drops an item. If the block break is cancelled, this event won't be called. If isDropItems in BlockBreakEvent is set to false, this event won't be called. This event will also be called if the player breaks a multi block structure, for example a torch on top of a stone. Both items will have an event call. The Block is already broken as this event is called, so #getBlock() will be AIR in most cases. Use #getBlockState() for more Information about the broken block.
events.name: BlockDropItemEvent

events.javadoc: Represents a block related event.
events.name: BlockEvent

events.javadoc: An event that's called when a block yields experience.
events.name: BlockExpEvent

events.javadoc: Called when a block explodes
events.name: BlockExplodeEvent

events.javadoc: Called when a block fades, melts or disappears based on world conditions Examples: Snow melting due to being near a light source. Ice melting due to being near a light source. Fire burning out after time, without destroying fuel block. Coral fading to dead coral due to lack of water Turtle Egg bursting when a turtle hatches If a Block Fade event is cancelled, the block will not fade, melt or disappear.
events.name: BlockFadeEvent

events.javadoc: Called with the block changes resulting from a player fertilizing a given block with bonemeal. Will be called after the applicable StructureGrowEvent.
events.name: BlockFertilizeEvent

events.javadoc: Called when a block is formed or spreads based on world conditions. Use BlockSpreadEvent to catch blocks that actually spread and don't just "randomly" form. Examples: Snow forming due to a snow storm. Ice forming in a snowy Biome like Taiga or Tundra. Obsidian / Cobblestone forming due to contact with water. Concrete forming due to mixing of concrete powder and water. If a Block Form event is cancelled, the block will not be formed.
events.name: BlockFormEvent

events.javadoc: Represents events with a source block and a destination block, currently only applies to liquid (lava and water) and teleporting dragon eggs. If a Block From To event is cancelled, the block will not move (the liquid will not flow).
events.name: BlockFromToEvent

events.javadoc: Called when a block grows naturally in the world. Examples: Wheat Sugar Cane Cactus Watermelon Pumpkin Turtle Egg If a Block Grow event is cancelled, the block will not grow.
events.name: BlockGrowEvent

events.javadoc: Called when a block is ignited. If you want to catch when a Player places fire, you need to use BlockPlaceEvent. If a Block Ignite event is cancelled, the block will not be ignited.
events.name: BlockIgniteEvent

events.javadoc: Fired when a single block placement action of a player triggers the creation of multiple blocks(e.g. placing a bed block). The block returned by BlockPlaceEvent.getBlockPlaced() and its related methods is the block where the placed block would exist if the placement only affected a single block.
events.name: BlockMultiPlaceEvent

events.javadoc: Thrown when a block physics check is called. This event is a high frequency event, it may be called thousands of times per a second on a busy server. Plugins are advised to listen to the event with caution and only perform lightweight checks when using it. In addition to this, cancelling the event is liable to leave the world in an inconsistent state. For example if you use the event to leave a block floating in mid air when that block has a requirement to be attached to something, there is no guarantee that the floating block will persist across server restarts or map upgrades. Plugins should also note that where possible this event may only called for the "root" block of physics updates in order to limit event spam. Physics updates that cause other blocks to change their state may not result in an event for each of those blocks (usually adjacent). If you are concerned about monitoring these changes then you should check adjacent blocks yourself.
events.name: BlockPhysicsEvent

events.javadoc: Called when a piston block is triggered
events.name: BlockPistonEvent

events.javadoc: Called when a piston extends
events.name: BlockPistonExtendEvent

events.javadoc: Called when a piston retracts
events.name: BlockPistonRetractEvent

events.javadoc: Called when a block is placed by a player. If a Block Place event is cancelled, the block will not be placed.
events.name: BlockPlaceEvent

events.javadoc: Called when a redstone current changes
events.name: BlockRedstoneEvent

events.javadoc: Event fired when a dispenser shears a nearby sheep.
events.name: BlockShearEntityEvent

events.javadoc: Called when a block spreads based on world conditions. Use BlockFormEvent to catch blocks that "randomly" form instead of actually spread. Examples: Mushrooms spreading. Fire spreading. If a Block Spread event is cancelled, the block will not spread.
events.name: BlockSpreadEvent

events.javadoc: Called when the brewing of the contents inside the Brewing Stand is complete.
events.name: BrewEvent

events.javadoc: Called when an ItemStack is about to increase the fuel level of a brewing stand.
events.name: BrewingStandFuelEvent

events.javadoc: Event triggered for server broadcast messages such as from Server.broadcast(String, String). This event behaves similarly to AsyncPlayerChatEvent in that it should be async if fired from an async thread. Please see that event for further information.
events.name: BroadcastMessageEvent

events.javadoc: Represents a Chunk related event
events.name: ChunkEvent

events.javadoc: Called when a chunk is loaded
events.name: ChunkLoadEvent

events.javadoc: Thrown when a new chunk has finished being populated. If your intent is to populate the chunk using this event, please see BlockPopulator
events.name: ChunkPopulateEvent

events.javadoc: Called when a chunk is unloaded
events.name: ChunkUnloadEvent

events.javadoc: Called when the recipe of an Item is completed inside a crafting matrix.
events.name: CraftItemEvent

events.javadoc: Called when a creature is spawned into a world. If a Creature Spawn event is cancelled, the creature will not spawn.
events.name: CreatureSpawnEvent

events.javadoc: Called when a Creeper is ignite flag is changed (armed/disarmed to explode).
events.name: CreeperIgniteEvent

events.javadoc: Called when a Creeper is struck by lightning. If a Creeper Power event is cancelled, the Creeper will not be powered.
events.name: CreeperPowerEvent

events.javadoc: Called when an ItemStack is successfully enchanted (currently at enchantment table)
events.name: EnchantItemEvent

events.javadoc: Called when an EnderDragon switches controller phase.
events.name: EnderDragonChangePhaseEvent

events.javadoc: Fired when a DragonFireball collides with a block/entity and spawns an AreaEffectCloud
events.name: EnderDragonFireballHitEvent

events.javadoc: Fired when an EnderDragon spawns an AreaEffectCloud by shooting flames
events.name: EnderDragonFlameEvent

events.javadoc: Fired when an EnderDragon shoots a fireball
events.name: EnderDragonShootFireballEvent

events.javadoc: Fired when an Enderman determines if it should attack a player or not. Starts off cancelled if the player is wearing a pumpkin head or is not looking at the Enderman, according to Vanilla rules.
events.name: EndermanAttackPlayerEvent

events.javadoc: Fired any time an entity is being added to the world for any reason. Not to be confused with CreatureSpawnEvent This will fire anytime a chunk is reloaded too.
events.name: EntityAddToWorldEvent

events.javadoc: Called when the amount of air an entity has remaining changes.
events.name: EntityAirChangeEvent

events.javadoc: Called when a block is formed by entities. Examples: Snow formed by a Snowman. Frosted Ice formed by the Frost Walker enchantment.
events.name: EntityBlockFormEvent

events.javadoc: Called when an Entity breaks a door Cancelling the event will cause the event to be delayed
events.name: EntityBreakDoorEvent

events.javadoc: Called when one Entity breeds with another Entity.
events.name: EntityBreedEvent

events.javadoc: Called when any Entity, excluding players, changes a block.
events.name: EntityChangeBlockEvent

events.javadoc: Called when a block causes an entity to combust.
events.name: EntityCombustByBlockEvent

events.javadoc: Called when an entity causes another entity to combust.
events.name: EntityCombustByEntityEvent

events.javadoc: Called when an entity combusts. If an Entity Combust event is cancelled, the entity will not combust.
events.name: EntityCombustEvent

events.javadoc: Deprecated. Use PortalCreateEventUse PortalCreateEventThrown when a Living Entity creates a portal in a world.
events.name: EntityCreatePortalEvent

events.javadoc: Called when an entity is damaged by a block
events.name: EntityDamageByBlockEvent

events.javadoc: Called when an entity is damaged by an entity
events.name: EntityDamageByEntityEvent

events.javadoc: Stores data for damage events
events.name: EntityDamageEvent

events.javadoc: Thrown whenever a LivingEntity dies
events.name: EntityDeathEvent

events.javadoc: Called when an entity stops riding another entity.
events.name: EntityDismountEvent

events.javadoc: Thrown when an entity creates an item drop.
events.name: EntityDropItemEvent

events.javadoc: Called when an Entity enters a block and is stored in that block. This event is called for bees entering a bee hive. It is not called when a silverfish "enters" a stone block. For that listen to the EntityChangeBlockEvent.
events.name: EntityEnterBlockEvent

events.javadoc: Represents an Entity-related event
events.name: EntityEvent

events.javadoc: Called when an entity explodes
events.name: EntityExplodeEvent

events.javadoc: Called when an entity interacts with an object
events.name: EntityInteractEvent

events.javadoc: Called when an entity jumps Cancelling the event will stop the entity from jumping
events.name: EntityJumpEvent

events.javadoc: Fired when an Entity is knocked back by the hit of another Entity. The acceleration vector can be modified. If this event is cancelled, the entity is not knocked back.
events.name: EntityKnockbackByEntityEvent

events.javadoc: Called when an entity attempts to ride another entity.
events.name: EntityMountEvent

events.javadoc: Fired when an Entity decides to start moving towards a location. This event does not fire for the entities actual movement. Only when it is choosing to start moving to a location.
events.name: EntityPathfindEvent

events.javadoc: Thrown when a entity picks an item up from the ground
events.name: EntityPickupItemEvent

events.javadoc: Deprecated. draft APIdraft APITriggered when a entity is created in the world by a player "placing" an item on a block. Note that this event is currently only fired for four specific placements: armor stands, boats, minecarts, and end crystals.
events.name: EntityPlaceEvent

events.javadoc: Called when an entity comes into contact with a portal
events.name: EntityPortalEnterEvent

events.javadoc: Called when a non-player entity is about to teleport because it is in contact with a portal. For players see PlayerPortalEvent
events.name: EntityPortalEvent

events.javadoc: Called before an entity exits a portal. This event allows you to modify the velocity of the entity after they have successfully exited the portal.
events.name: EntityPortalExitEvent

events.javadoc: Called when an entity changes its pose.
events.name: EntityPoseChangeEvent

events.javadoc: Called when a potion effect is modified on an entity. If the event is cancelled, no change will be made on the entity.
events.name: EntityPotionEffectEvent

events.javadoc: Stores data for health-regain events
events.name: EntityRegainHealthEvent

events.javadoc: Fired any time an entity is being removed from a world for any reason
events.name: EntityRemoveFromWorldEvent

events.javadoc: Called when an entity dies and may have the opportunity to be resurrected. Will be called in a cancelled state if the entity does not have a totem equipped.
events.name: EntityResurrectEvent

events.javadoc: Called when a LivingEntity shoots a bow firing an arrow
events.name: EntityShootBowEvent

events.javadoc: Called when an entity is spawned into a world. If an Entity Spawn event is cancelled, the entity will not spawn.
events.name: EntitySpawnEvent

events.javadoc: Thrown when a LivingEntity is tamed
events.name: EntityTameEvent

events.javadoc: Called when a creature targets or untargets another entity
events.name: EntityTargetEvent

events.javadoc: Called when an Entity targets a LivingEntity and can only target LivingEntity's.
events.name: EntityTargetLivingEntityEvent

events.javadoc: Fired any time an entity attempts to teleport in an end gateway
events.name: EntityTeleportEndGatewayEvent

events.javadoc: Thrown when a non-player entity is teleported from one location to another. This may be as a result of natural causes (Enderman, Shulker), pathfinding (Wolf), or commands (/teleport).
events.name: EntityTeleportEvent

events.javadoc: Sent when an entity's gliding status is toggled with an Elytra. Examples of when this event would be called: Player presses the jump key while in midair and using an Elytra Player lands on ground while they are gliding (with an Elytra) This can be visually estimated by the animation in which a player turns horizontal.
events.name: EntityToggleGlideEvent

events.javadoc: Sent when an entity's swimming status is toggled.
events.name: EntityToggleSwimEvent

events.javadoc: Fired when an entity transforms into another entity If the event is cancelled, the entity will not transform
events.name: EntityTransformedEvent

events.javadoc: Called when an entity is about to be replaced by another entity.
events.name: EntityTransformEvent

events.javadoc: Called immediately prior to an entity being unleashed.
events.name: EntityUnleashEvent

events.javadoc: Fired when lightning strikes an entity
events.name: EntityZapEvent

events.javadoc: Represents an event. All events require a static method named getHandlerList() which returns the same HandlerList as getHandlers().
events.name: Event

events.javadoc: Called when a ThrownExpBottle hits and releases experience.
events.name: ExpBottleEvent

events.javadoc: Fired anytime the server is about to merge 2 experience orbs into one
events.name: ExperienceOrbMergeEvent

events.javadoc: Called when an entity has made a decision to explode.
events.name: ExplosionPrimeEvent

events.javadoc: Fired once a profiles additional properties (such as textures) has been filled
events.name: FillProfileEvent

events.javadoc: Called when a firework explodes.
events.name: FireworkExplodeEvent

events.javadoc: Called when the fluid level of a block changes due to changes in adjacent blocks.
events.name: FluidLevelChangeEvent

events.javadoc: Called when a human entity's food level changes
events.name: FoodLevelChangeEvent

events.javadoc: Called when an ItemStack is successfully burned as fuel in a furnace.
events.name: FurnaceBurnEvent

events.javadoc: This event is called when a player takes items out of the furnace
events.name: FurnaceExtractEvent

events.javadoc: Called when an ItemStack is successfully smelted in a furnace.
events.name: FurnaceSmeltEvent

events.javadoc: This event is fired if server is getting queried over GS4 Query protocol Adapted from Velocity's ProxyQueryEvent
events.name: GS4QueryEvent

events.javadoc: Triggered when a hanging entity is removed by an entity
events.name: HangingBreakByEntityEvent

events.javadoc: Triggered when a hanging entity is removed
events.name: HangingBreakEvent

events.javadoc: Represents a hanging entity-related event.
events.name: HangingEvent

events.javadoc: Triggered when a hanging entity is created in the world
events.name: HangingPlaceEvent

events.javadoc: Called when a horse jumps.
events.name: HorseJumpEvent

events.javadoc: This event is called when a player clicks a slot in an inventory. Because InventoryClickEvent occurs within a modification of the Inventory, not all Inventory related methods are safe to use. The following should never be invoked by an EventHandler for InventoryClickEvent using the HumanEntity or InventoryView associated with this event: HumanEntity.closeInventory() HumanEntity.openInventory(Inventory) HumanEntity.openWorkbench(Location, boolean) HumanEntity.openEnchanting(Location, boolean) InventoryView.close() To invoke one of these methods, schedule a task using BukkitScheduler.runTask(Plugin, Runnable), which will run the task on the next tick. Also be aware that this is not an exhaustive list, and other methods could potentially create issues as well. Assuming the EntityHuman associated with this event is an instance of a Player, manipulating the MaxStackSize or contents of an Inventory will require an Invocation of Player.updateInventory(). Modifications to slots that are modified by the results of this InventoryClickEvent can be overwritten. To change these slots, this event should be cancelled and all desired changes to the inventory applied. Alternatively, scheduling a task using BukkitScheduler.runTask( Plugin, Runnable), which would execute the task on the next tick, would work as well.
events.name: InventoryClickEvent

events.javadoc: Represents a player related inventory event
events.name: InventoryCloseEvent

events.javadoc: This event is called when a player in creative mode puts down or picks up an item in their inventory / hotbar and when they drop items from their Inventory while in creative mode.
events.name: InventoryCreativeEvent

events.javadoc: This event is called when the player drags an item in their cursor across the inventory. The ItemStack is distributed across the slots the HumanEntity dragged over. The method of distribution is described by the DragType returned by getType(). Canceling this event will result in none of the changes described in getNewItems() being applied to the Inventory. Because InventoryDragEvent occurs within a modification of the Inventory, not all Inventory related methods are safe to use. The following should never be invoked by an EventHandler for InventoryDragEvent using the HumanEntity or InventoryView associated with this event. HumanEntity.closeInventory() HumanEntity.openInventory(Inventory) HumanEntity.openWorkbench(Location, boolean) HumanEntity.openEnchanting(Location, boolean) InventoryView.close() To invoke one of these methods, schedule a task using BukkitScheduler.runTask(Plugin, Runnable), which will run the task on the next tick. Also be aware that this is not an exhaustive list, and other methods could potentially create issues as well. Assuming the EntityHuman associated with this event is an instance of a Player, manipulating the MaxStackSize or contents of an Inventory will require an Invocation of Player.updateInventory(). Any modifications to slots that are modified by the results of this InventoryDragEvent will be overwritten. To change these slots, this event should be cancelled and the changes applied. Alternatively, scheduling a task using BukkitScheduler.runTask(Plugin, Runnable), which would execute the task on the next tick, would work as well.
events.name: InventoryDragEvent

events.javadoc: Represents a player related inventory event
events.name: InventoryEvent

events.javadoc: An abstract base class for events that describe an interaction between a HumanEntity and the contents of an Inventory.
events.name: InventoryInteractEvent

events.javadoc: Called when some entity or block (e.g. hopper) tries to move items directly from one inventory to another. When this event is called, the initiator may already have removed the item from the source inventory and is ready to move it into the destination inventory. If this event is cancelled, the items will be returned to the source inventory, if needed. If this event is not cancelled, the initiator will try to put the ItemStack into the destination inventory. If this is not possible and the ItemStack has not been modified, the source inventory slot will be restored to its former state. Otherwise any additional items will be discarded.
events.name: InventoryMoveItemEvent

events.javadoc: Represents a player related inventory event
events.name: InventoryOpenEvent

events.javadoc: Called when a hopper or hopper minecart picks up a dropped item.
events.name: InventoryPickupItemEvent

events.javadoc: This event is called when a Item is removed from the world because it has existed for 5 minutes. Cancelling the event results in the item being allowed to exist for 5 more minutes. This behavior is not guaranteed and may change in future versions.
events.name: ItemDespawnEvent

events.javadoc: Called when an item is spawned into a world
events.name: ItemSpawnEvent

events.javadoc: Called when leaves are decaying naturally. If a Leaves Decay event is cancelled, the leaves will not decay.
events.name: LeavesDecayEvent

events.javadoc: Stores data for lightning striking
events.name: LightningStrikeEvent

events.javadoc: Called when a splash potion hits an area
events.name: LingeringPotionSplashEvent

events.javadoc: Allows a plugin to be notified anytime AFTER a Profile has been looked up from the Mojang API This is an opportunity to view the response and potentially cache things. No guarantees are made about thread execution context for this event. If you need to know, check event.isAsync()
events.name: LookupProfileEvent

events.javadoc: Called when a LootTable is generated in the world for an InventoryHolder. This event is NOT currently called when an entity's loot table has been generated (use EntityDeathEvent.getDrops(), but WILL be called by plugins invoking LootTable.fillInventory(org.bukkit.inventory.Inventory, java.util.Random, LootContext).
events.name: LootGenerateEvent

events.javadoc: Called when a map is initialized.
events.name: MapInitializeEvent

events.javadoc: Called when the moisture level of a soil block changes.
events.name: MoistureChangeEvent

events.javadoc: Called when a note block is being played through player interaction or a redstone current.
events.name: NotePlayEvent

events.javadoc: Extended version of ServerListPingEvent that allows full control of the response sent to the client.
events.name: PaperServerListPingEvent

events.javadoc: Called when a phantom is spawned for an exhausted player
events.name: PhantomPreSpawnEvent

events.javadoc: Stores data for pigs being zapped
events.name: PigZapEvent

events.javadoc: Called when a Pig Zombie is angered by another entity. If the event is cancelled, the pig zombie will not be angered.
events.name: PigZombieAngerEvent

events.javadoc: Called when a player is granted a criteria in an advancement.
events.name: PlayerAdvancementCriterionGrantEvent

events.javadoc: Called when a player has completed all criteria in an advancement.
events.name: PlayerAdvancementDoneEvent

events.javadoc: Represents a player animation event
events.name: PlayerAnimationEvent

events.javadoc: Called when the player themselves change their armor items Not currently called for environmental factors though it MAY BE IN THE FUTURE
events.name: PlayerArmorChangeEvent

events.javadoc: Called when a player interacts with an armor stand and will either swap, retrieve or place an item.
events.name: PlayerArmorStandManipulateEvent

events.javadoc: Called when processing a player's attack on an entity when the player's attack strength cooldown is reset
events.name: PlayerAttackEntityCooldownResetEvent

events.javadoc: This event is fired when the player is almost about to enter the bed.
events.name: PlayerBedEnterEvent

events.javadoc: This event is fired when the player is leaving a bed.
events.name: PlayerBedLeaveEvent

events.javadoc: Called when a player empties a bucket
events.name: PlayerBucketEmptyEvent

events.javadoc: Called when a player interacts with a Bucket
events.name: PlayerBucketEvent

events.javadoc: Called when a player fills a bucket
events.name: PlayerBucketFillEvent

events.javadoc: Called when a player changes their main hand in the client settings.
events.name: PlayerChangedMainHandEvent

events.javadoc: Called when a player switches to another world.
events.name: PlayerChangedWorldEvent

events.javadoc: This event is called after a player registers or unregisters a new plugin channel.
events.name: PlayerChannelEvent

events.javadoc: Deprecated. This event will fire from the main thread and allows the use of all of the Bukkit API, unlike the AsyncPlayerChatEvent. Listening to this event forces chat to wait for the main thread which causes delays for chat. AsyncPlayerChatEvent is the encouraged alternative for thread safe implementations.This event will fire from the main thread and allows the use of all of the Bukkit API, unlike the AsyncPlayerChatEvent. Listening to this event forces chat to wait for the main thread which causes delays for chat. AsyncPlayerChatEvent is the encouraged alternative for thread safe implementations.Holds information for player chat and commands
events.name: PlayerChatEvent

events.javadoc: Deprecated. This event is no longer fired due to client changesThis event is no longer fired due to client changesCalled when a player attempts to tab-complete a chat message.
events.name: PlayerChatTabCompleteEvent

events.javadoc: Called when the player changes his client settings
events.name: PlayerClientOptionsChangeEvent

events.javadoc: This event is called whenever a player runs a command (by placing a slash at the start of their message). It is called early in the command handling process, and modifications in this event (via setMessage(String)) will be shown in the behavior. Many plugins will have no use for this event, and you should attempt to avoid using it if it is not necessary. Some examples of valid uses for this event are: Logging executed commands to a separate file Variable substitution. For example, replacing ${nearbyPlayer} with the name of the nearest other player, or simulating the @a and @p decorators used by Command Blocks in plugins that do not handle it. Conditionally blocking commands belonging to other plugins. For example, blocking the use of the /home command in a combat arena. Per-sender command aliases. For example, after a player runs the command /calias cr gamemode creative, the next time they run /cr, it gets replaced into /gamemode creative. (Global command aliases should be done by registering the alias.) Examples of incorrect uses are: Using this event to run command logic If the event is cancelled, processing of the command will halt. The state of whether or not there is a slash (/) at the beginning of the message should be preserved. If a slash is added or removed, unexpected behavior may result.
events.name: PlayerCommandPreprocessEvent

events.javadoc: This event is called when the list of available server commands is sent to the player. Commands may be removed from display using this event, but implementations are not required to securely remove all traces of the command. If secure removal of commands is required, then the command should be assigned a permission which is not granted to the player.
events.name: PlayerCommandSendEvent

events.javadoc: This event is invoked when a player has disconnected. It is guaranteed that, if the server is in online-mode, that the provided uuid and username have been validated. The event is invoked for players who have not yet logged into the world, whereas PlayerQuitEvent is only invoked on players who have logged into the world. The event is invoked for players who have already logged into the world, although whether or not the player exists in the world at the time of firing is undefined. (That is, whether the plugin can retrieve a Player object using the event parameters is undefined). However, it is guaranteed that this event is invoked AFTER PlayerQuitEvent, if the player has already logged into the world. This event is guaranteed to never fire unless AsyncPlayerPreLoginEvent has been fired beforehand, and this event may not be called in parallel with AsyncPlayerPreLoginEvent for the same connection. Cancelling the AsyncPlayerPreLoginEvent guarantees the corresponding PlayerConnectionCloseEvent is never called. The event may be invoked asynchronously or synchronously. Plugins should check Event.isAsynchronous() and handle accordingly.
events.name: PlayerConnectionCloseEvent

events.javadoc: Thrown whenever a Player dies
events.name: PlayerDeathEvent

events.javadoc: Thrown when a player drops an item from their inventory
events.name: PlayerDropItemEvent

events.javadoc: Called when a player edits or signs a book and quill item. If the event is cancelled, no changes are made to the BookMeta
events.name: PlayerEditBookEvent

events.javadoc: Called when a player throws an egg and it might hatch
events.name: PlayerEggThrowEvent

events.javadoc: Fired when a player boosts elytra flight with a firework
events.name: PlayerElytraBoostEvent

events.javadoc: Represents a player related event
events.name: PlayerEvent

events.javadoc: Called when a players experience changes naturally
events.name: PlayerExpChangeEvent

events.javadoc: Thrown when a player is fishing
events.name: PlayerFishEvent

events.javadoc: Called when the GameMode of the player is changed.
events.name: PlayerGameModeChangeEvent

events.javadoc: This event is fired during a player handshake. If there are no listeners listening to this event, the logic default to your server platform will be ran. WARNING: TAMPERING WITH THIS EVENT CAN BE DANGEROUS
events.name: PlayerHandshakeEvent

events.javadoc: Represents an event that is called when a player right clicks an entity that also contains the location where the entity was clicked. Note that the client may sometimes spuriously send this packet in addition to PlayerInteractEntityEvent. Users are advised to listen to this (parent) class unless specifically required.
events.name: PlayerInteractAtEntityEvent

events.javadoc: Represents an event that is called when a player right clicks an entity.
events.name: PlayerInteractEntityEvent

events.javadoc: Represents an event that is called when a player interacts with an object or air, potentially fired once for each hand. The hand can be determined using getHand(). This event will fire as cancelled if the vanilla behavior is to do nothing (e.g interacting with air). For the purpose of avoiding doubt, this means that the event will only be in the cancelled state if it is fired as a result of some prediction made by the server where no subsequent code will run, rather than when the subsequent interaction activity (e.g. placing a block in an illegal position (BlockCanBuildEvent) will fail.
events.name: PlayerInteractEvent

events.javadoc: Fired when a player's item breaks (such as a shovel or flint and steel). The item that's breaking will exist in the inventory with a stack size of 0. After the event, the item's durability will be reset to 0.
events.name: PlayerItemBreakEvent

events.javadoc: This event will fire when a player is finishing consuming an item (food, potion, milk bucket). If the ItemStack is modified the server will use the effects of the new item and not remove the original one from the player's inventory. If the event is cancelled the effect will not be applied and the item will not be removed from the player's inventory.
events.name: PlayerItemConsumeEvent

events.javadoc: Called when an item used by the player takes durability damage as a result of being used.
events.name: PlayerItemDamageEvent

events.javadoc: Fired when a player changes their currently held item
events.name: PlayerItemHeldEvent

events.javadoc: Represents when a player has an item repaired via the Mending enchantment. This event is fired directly before the PlayerExpChangeEvent, and the results of this event directly affect the PlayerExpChangeEvent.
events.name: PlayerItemMendEvent

events.javadoc: Called when a player joins a server
events.name: PlayerJoinEvent

events.javadoc: Called when the server detects the player is jumping. Added to avoid the overhead and special case logic that many plugins use when checking for jumps via PlayerMoveEvent, this event is fired whenever the server detects that the player is jumping.
events.name: PlayerJumpEvent

events.javadoc: Called when a player gets kicked from the server
events.name: PlayerKickEvent

events.javadoc: Called when a player shoots a projectile
events.name: PlayerLaunchProjectileEvent

events.javadoc: Called immediately prior to a creature being leashed by a player.
events.name: PlayerLeashEntityEvent

events.javadoc: Called when a players level changes
events.name: PlayerLevelChangeEvent

events.javadoc: Called when the locale of the player is changed.
events.name: PlayerLocaleChangeEvent

events.javadoc: Called when a player changes their locale in the client settings.
events.name: PlayerLocaleChangeEvent

events.javadoc: Stores details for players attempting to log in. Note that this event is called early in the player initialization process. It is recommended that most options involving the Player entity be postponed to the PlayerJoinEvent instead.
events.name: PlayerLoginEvent

events.javadoc: Holds information for player movement events
events.name: PlayerMoveEvent

events.javadoc: Fired when the server is calculating what chunks to try to spawn monsters in every Monster Spawn Tick event
events.name: PlayerNaturallySpawnCreaturesEvent

events.javadoc: Thrown when a player picks up an arrow from the ground.
events.name: PlayerPickupArrowEvent

events.javadoc: Fired when a player is attempting to pick up an experience orb
events.name: PlayerPickupExperienceEvent

events.javadoc: Deprecated. EntityPickupItemEventEntityPickupItemEventThrown when a player picks an item up from the ground
events.name: PlayerPickupItemEvent

events.javadoc: Called when a player is about to teleport because it is in contact with a portal. For other entities see EntityPortalEvent
events.name: PlayerPortalEvent

events.javadoc: Fired after a player has respawned
events.name: PlayerPostRespawnEvent

events.javadoc: Deprecated. This event causes synchronization from the login thread; AsyncPlayerPreLoginEvent is preferred to keep the secondary threads asynchronous.This event causes synchronization from the login thread; AsyncPlayerPreLoginEvent is preferred to keep the secondary threads asynchronous.Stores details for players attempting to log in
events.name: PlayerPreLoginEvent

events.javadoc: Called when a player leaves a server
events.name: PlayerQuitEvent

events.javadoc: Called when a player is firing a bow and the server is choosing an arrow to use.
events.name: PlayerReadyArrowEvent

events.javadoc: Called when a player clicks a recipe in the recipe book
events.name: PlayerRecipeBookClickEvent

events.javadoc: Called when a player discovers a new recipe in the recipe book.
events.name: PlayerRecipeDiscoverEvent

events.javadoc: This is called immediately after a player registers for a plugin channel.
events.name: PlayerRegisterChannelEvent

events.javadoc: Called when a player takes action on a resource pack request sent via Player.setResourcePack(java.lang.String).
events.name: PlayerResourcePackStatusEvent

events.javadoc: Called when a player respawns.
events.name: PlayerRespawnEvent

events.javadoc: This event is fired when the player activates the riptide enchantment, using their trident to propel them through the air. N.B. the riptide action is currently performed client side, so manipulating the player in this event may have undesired effects.
events.name: PlayerRiptideEvent

events.javadoc: Called when a player shears an entity
events.name: PlayerShearEntityEvent

events.javadoc: Called when player is about to spawn in a world after joining the server.
events.name: PlayerSpawnLocationEvent

events.javadoc: Triggered when a player starts spectating an entity in spectator mode.
events.name: PlayerStartSpectatingEntityEvent

events.javadoc: Called when a player statistic is incremented. This event is not called for some high frequency statistics, e.g. movement based statistics.
events.name: PlayerStatisticIncrementEvent

events.javadoc: Triggered when a player stops spectating an entity in spectator mode.
events.name: PlayerStopSpectatingEntityEvent

events.javadoc: Called when a player swap items between main hand and off hand using the hotkey.
events.name: PlayerSwapHandItemsEvent

events.javadoc: This event is called when a player clicks the button to take a book of a Lectern. If this event is cancelled the book remains on the lectern.
events.name: PlayerTakeLecternBookEvent

events.javadoc: Fired when a teleport is triggered for an End Gateway
events.name: PlayerTeleportEndGatewayEvent

events.javadoc: Holds information for player teleport events
events.name: PlayerTeleportEvent

events.javadoc: Called when a player toggles their flying state
events.name: PlayerToggleFlightEvent

events.javadoc: Called when a player toggles their sneaking state
events.name: PlayerToggleSneakEvent

events.javadoc: Called when a player toggles their sprinting state
events.name: PlayerToggleSprintEvent

events.javadoc: Called prior to an entity being unleashed due to a player's action.
events.name: PlayerUnleashEntityEvent

events.javadoc: This is called immediately after a player unregisters for a plugin channel.
events.name: PlayerUnregisterChannelEvent

events.javadoc: Called when the velocity of a player changes.
events.name: PlayerVelocityEvent

events.javadoc: Called when a plugin is disabled.
events.name: PluginDisableEvent

events.javadoc: Called when a plugin is enabled.
events.name: PluginEnableEvent

events.javadoc: Used for plugin enable and disable events
events.name: PluginEvent

events.javadoc: Called when a portal is created
events.name: PortalCreateEvent

events.javadoc: Called when a splash potion hits an area
events.name: PotionSplashEvent

events.javadoc: WARNING: This event only fires for a limited number of cases, and not for every case that CreatureSpawnEvent does. You should still listen to CreatureSpawnEvent as a backup, and only use this event as an "enhancement". The intent of this event is to improve server performance, so limited use cases. Currently: NATURAL and SPAWNER based reasons. Please submit a Pull Request for future additions. Also, Plugins that replace Entity Registrations with their own custom entities might not fire this event.
events.name: PreCreatureSpawnEvent

events.javadoc: Fired when the server is requesting to fill in properties of an incomplete profile, such as textures. Allows plugins to pre populate cached properties and avoid a call to the Mojang API
events.name: PreFillProfileEvent

events.javadoc: Allows a plugin to intercept a Profile Lookup for a Profile by name At the point of event fire, the UUID and properties are unset. If a plugin sets the UUID, and optionally the properties, the API call to look up the profile may be skipped. No guarantees are made about thread execution context for this event. If you need to know, check event.isAsync()
events.name: PreLookupProfileEvent

events.javadoc: Called when an item is put in a slot for repair by an anvil.
events.name: PrepareAnvilEvent

events.javadoc: Called when an item is put in a slot for grinding in a Grindstone
events.name: PrepareGrindstoneEvent

events.javadoc: Called when an ItemStack is inserted in an enchantment table - can be called multiple times
events.name: PrepareItemEnchantEvent

events.javadoc: Called before an entity is spawned into a world by a spawner. This only includes the spawner's location and not the full BlockState snapshot for performance reasons. If you really need it you have to get the spawner yourself.
events.name: PreSpawnerSpawnEvent

events.javadoc: Fires when the server needs to verify if a player is whitelisted. Plugins may override/control the servers whitelist with this event, and dynamically change the kick message.
events.name: ProfileWhitelistVerifyEvent

events.javadoc: Called when an projectile collides with an entity This event is called before EntityDamageByEntityEvent, and cancelling it will allow the projectile to continue flying
events.name: ProjectileCollideEvent

events.javadoc: Called when a projectile hits an object
events.name: ProjectileHitEvent

events.javadoc: Called when a projectile is launched.
events.name: ProjectileLaunchEvent

events.javadoc: Represents events related to raids.
events.name: RaidEvent

events.javadoc: This event is called when a Raid was complete with a clear result.
events.name: RaidFinishEvent

events.javadoc: Called when a raid wave spawns.
events.name: RaidSpawnWaveEvent

events.javadoc: Called when a Raid is stopped.
events.name: RaidStopEvent

events.javadoc: Called when a Raid is triggered (e.g: a player with Bad Omen effect enters a village).
events.name: RaidTriggerEvent

events.javadoc: This event is called when a command is received over RCON. See the javadocs of ServerCommandEvent for more information.
events.name: RemoteServerCommandEvent

events.javadoc: This event is called when a command is run by a non-player. It is called early in the command handling process, and modifications in this event (via setCommand(String)) will be shown in the behavior. Many plugins will have no use for this event, and you should attempt to avoid using it if it is not necessary. Some examples of valid uses for this event are: Logging executed commands to a separate file Variable substitution. For example, replacing ${ip:Steve} with the connection IP of the player named Steve, or simulating the @a and @p decorators used by Command Blocks for plugins that do not handle it. Conditionally blocking commands belonging to other plugins. Per-sender command aliases. For example, after the console runs the command /calias cr gamemode creative, the next time they run /cr, it gets replaced into /gamemode creative. (Global command aliases should be done by registering the alias.) Examples of incorrect uses are: Using this event to run command logic If the event is cancelled, processing of the command will halt. The state of whether or not there is a slash (/) at the beginning of the message should be preserved. If a slash is added or removed, unexpected behavior may result.
events.name: ServerCommandEvent

events.javadoc: Miscellaneous server events
events.name: ServerEvent

events.javadoc: Called whenever an exception is thrown in a recoverable section of the server.
events.name: ServerExceptionEvent

events.javadoc: Called when a server list ping is coming in. Displayed players can be checked and removed by iterating over this event.
events.name: ServerListPingEvent

events.javadoc: This event is called when either the server startup or reload has completed.
events.name: ServerLoadEvent

events.javadoc: Called when the server has finished ticking the main loop
events.name: ServerTickEndEvent

events.javadoc: An event relating to a registered service. This is called in a ServicesManager
events.name: ServiceEvent

events.javadoc: This event is called when a service is registered. Warning: The order in which register and unregister events are called should not be relied upon.
events.name: ServiceRegisterEvent

events.javadoc: This event is called when a service is unregistered. Warning: The order in which register and unregister events are called should not be relied upon.
events.name: ServiceUnregisterEvent

events.javadoc: Called when a sheep's wool is dyed
events.name: SheepDyeWoolEvent

events.javadoc: Called when a sheep regrows its wool
events.name: SheepRegrowWoolEvent

events.javadoc: Called when a sign is changed by a player. If a Sign Change event is cancelled, the sign will not be changed.
events.name: SignChangeEvent

events.javadoc: Event called when a player gets close to a skeleton horse and triggers the lightning trap
events.name: SkeletonHorseTrapEvent

events.javadoc: Fired when a Slime decides to change it's facing direction. This event does not fire for the entity's actual movement. Only when it is choosing to change direction.
events.name: SlimeChangeDirectionEvent

events.javadoc: Fired when a Slime decides to start pathfinding. This event does not fire for the entity's actual movement. Only when it is choosing to start moving.
events.name: SlimePathfindEvent

events.javadoc: Called when a Slime splits into smaller Slimes upon death
events.name: SlimeSplitEvent

events.javadoc: Fired when a Slime decides to start jumping while swimming in water/lava. This event does not fire for the entity's actual movement. Only when it is choosing to start jumping.
events.name: SlimeSwimEvent

events.javadoc: Fired when a Slime decides to change direction to target a LivingEntity. This event does not fire for the entity's actual movement. Only when it is choosing to start moving.
events.name: SlimeTargetLivingEntityEvent

events.javadoc: Fired when a Slime decides to start wandering. This event does not fire for the entity's actual movement. Only when it is choosing to start moving.
events.name: SlimeWanderEvent

events.javadoc: An event that is called when a world's spawn changes. The world's previous spawn location is included.
events.name: SpawnChangeEvent

events.javadoc: Called when an entity is spawned into a world by a spawner. If a Spawner Spawn event is cancelled, the entity will not spawn.
events.name: SpawnerSpawnEvent

events.javadoc: Called when a sponge absorbs water from the world. The world will be in its previous state, and getBlocks() will represent the changes to be made to the world, if the event is not cancelled. As this is a physics based event it may be called multiple times for "the same" changes.
events.name: SpongeAbsorbEvent

events.javadoc: Event that is called when an organic structure attempts to grow (Sapling -> Tree), (Mushroom -> Huge Mushroom), naturally or using bonemeal.
events.name: StructureGrowEvent

events.javadoc: Called when a CommandSender of any description (ie: player or console) attempts to tab complete. Note that due to client changes, if the sender is a Player, this event will only begin to fire once command arguments are specified, not commands themselves. Plugins wishing to remove commands from tab completion are advised to ensure the client does not have permission for the relevant commands, or use PlayerCommandSendEvent.
events.name: TabCompleteEvent

events.javadoc: Called when a thrown egg might hatch. This event fires for all thrown eggs that may hatch, players, dispensers, etc.
events.name: ThrownEggHatchEvent

events.javadoc: Stores data for thunder state changing in a world
events.name: ThunderChangeEvent

events.javadoc: Called when the time skips in a world. If the event is cancelled the time will not change.
events.name: TimeSkipEvent

events.javadoc: Called when TNT block is about to turn into TNTPrimed Cancelling it won't turn TNT into TNTPrimed and leaves the TNT block as-is
events.name: TNTPrimeEvent

events.javadoc: This event is called whenever a player clicks a new trade on the trades sidebar. This event allows the user to get the index of the trade, letting them get the MerchantRecipe via the Merchant.
events.name: TradeSelectEvent

events.javadoc: Fired when a Turtle decides to go home
events.name: TurtleGoHomeEvent

events.javadoc: Fired when a Turtle lays eggs
events.name: TurtleLayEggEvent

events.javadoc: Fired when a Turtle starts digging to lay eggs
events.name: TurtleStartDiggingEvent

events.javadoc: Raised when a vehicle collides with a block.
events.name: VehicleBlockCollisionEvent

events.javadoc: Raised when a vehicle collides.
events.name: VehicleCollisionEvent

events.javadoc: Raised when a vehicle is created.
events.name: VehicleCreateEvent

events.javadoc: Raised when a vehicle receives damage.
events.name: VehicleDamageEvent

events.javadoc: Raised when a vehicle is destroyed, which could be caused by either a player or the environment. This is not raised if the boat is simply 'removed' due to other means.
events.name: VehicleDestroyEvent

events.javadoc: Raised when an entity enters a vehicle.
events.name: VehicleEnterEvent

events.javadoc: Raised when a vehicle collides with an entity.
events.name: VehicleEntityCollisionEvent

events.javadoc: Represents a vehicle-related event.
events.name: VehicleEvent

events.javadoc: Raised when a living entity exits a vehicle.
events.name: VehicleExitEvent

events.javadoc: Raised when a vehicle moves.
events.name: VehicleMoveEvent

events.javadoc: Called when a vehicle updates
events.name: VehicleUpdateEvent

events.javadoc: Called whenever a villager acquires a new trade.
events.name: VillagerAcquireTradeEvent

events.javadoc: Called when a villager's trade's maximum uses is increased, due to a player's trade.
events.name: VillagerReplenishTradeEvent

events.javadoc: Stores data for weather changing in a world
events.name: WeatherChangeEvent

events.javadoc: Represents a Weather-related event
events.name: WeatherEvent

events.javadoc: This event is fired when whitelist is toggled
events.name: WhitelistToggleEvent

events.javadoc: Fired when a witch consumes the potion in their hand to buff themselves.
events.name: WitchConsumePotionEvent

events.javadoc: Fired when a witch throws a potion at a player
events.name: WitchThrowPotionEvent

events.javadoc: Represents events within a world
events.name: WorldEvent

events.javadoc: Called when a World is initializing
events.name: WorldInitEvent

events.javadoc: Called when a World is loaded
events.name: WorldLoadEvent

events.javadoc: Called when a World is saved.
events.name: WorldSaveEvent

events.javadoc: Called when a World is unloaded
events.name: WorldUnloadEvent
